---
title: "Veille arXiv"
date: today
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD"
---

# Veille arXiv

## Catégories surveillées

| Catégorie arXiv | Description | Fréquence de scan |
|-----------------|-------------|-------------------|
| `cs.AI` | Artificial Intelligence | Quotidien |
| `cs.CL` | Computation and Language (NLP) | Quotidien |
| `cs.LG` | Machine Learning | Quotidien |
| `cs.MA` | Multi-Agent Systems | Quotidien |
| `cs.SE` | Software Engineering | Quotidien |
| `stat.ML` | Machine Learning (Stats) | Quotidien |

## Filtres de pertinence

Le script `scripts/veille_arxiv.py` filtre les papers selon :

| Filtre | Mots-clés | Action |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| Agentic | `agent`, `agentic`, `autonomous`, `tool-use` | Priorité haute |
| LLM | `LLM`, `language model`, `transformer`, `GPT`, `Claude` | Priorité haute |
| Sandbox | `sandbox`, `isolation`, `container`, `microVM` | Priorité moyenne |
| RAG | `RAG`, `retrieval augmented`, `GraphRAG`, `knowledge graph` | Priorité haute |
| MCP | `MCP`, `model context protocol` | Priorité haute |
| Harness | `code agent`, `IDE`, `coding assistant` | Priorité moyenne |
| Benchmark | `benchmark`, `evaluation`, `leaderboard` | Priorité moyenne |

## Format de sortie

Chaque paper détecté génère une entrée dans `content/veille/arxiv-feed.qmd` :

```markdown
### [Titre du paper]
- **arXiv** : 2507.17311
- **Date** : 2025-07-23
- **Auteurs** : Intern-AI et al.
- **Catégorie** : cs.AI
- **Tags** : agentic, climate, research
- **Résumé** : Premier self-evolving AI agent pour climate science...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.17311
```

## Derniers papers détectés

::: callout-note
## Section auto-générée
Cette section est automatiquement mise à jour par le script `scripts/veille_arxiv.py` via GitHub Actions. Les entrées ci-dessous sont générées à chaque exécution.
:::

<!-- ARXIV_FEED_START -->
### VEXAIoT: Autonomous IoT Vulnerability EXploitation using AI Agents
- **arXiv** : 2607.09653v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-10
- **Auteurs** : Katherine Swinea, Kshitiz Aryal, Lopamudra Praharaj, Maanak Gupta
- **Score** : 12
- **Résumé** : Internet of Things (IoT) systems are inherently vulnerable due to constrained hardware, outdated firmware, and insecure default configurations, creating a need for scalable and adaptive security testing approaches. While recent adoptions of Large Language Model (LLM) agents have demonstrated promise...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09653v1

### Failure as a Process: An Anatomy of CLI Coding Agent Trajectories
- **arXiv** : 2607.09510v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-10
- **Auteurs** : Xiangxin Zhao, Han Li, Shuaiting Li, Tianyi Zhao, Earl T. Barr
- **Score** : 12
- **Résumé** : Large language model (LLM) coding agents are increasingly deployed to autonomously perform software engineering tasks in terminal-based environments, making their reliability a growing concern. Existing empirical studies investigate why coding agents fail, yet they largely treat failure as a final o...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09510v1

### Shared Selective Persistent Memory for Agentic LLM Systems
- **arXiv** : 2607.09493v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-10
- **Auteurs** : Sanjana Pedada, Aditya Dhavala, Neelraj Patil
- **Score** : 12
- **Résumé** : Agentic LLM systems that generate code through multi-turn tool use face a fundamental context problem: each session starts from zero, discarding the configuration choices, domain constraints, data schemas, and tool-use patterns that made previous sessions productive. Naively persisting entire conver...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09493v1

### Agora: Enhancing LLM Agent Reasoning Via Auction-Based Task Allocation
- **arXiv** : 2607.09600v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-10
- **Auteurs** : Kaiji Zhou, Ales Leonardis, Yue Feng
- **Score** : 9
- **Résumé** : Enhancing the reasoning capabilities of large language model (LLM) agents requires effective orchestration of diverse expert models and tools. However, existing frameworks typically call APIs based on coarse-grained matching between tasks and the functions of expert models or tools, while overlookin...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09600v1

### LLM for EDA in Front-End Design: Challenges and Opportunities
- **arXiv** : 2607.09616v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-10
- **Auteurs** : Kangwei Xu, Bing Li, Ulf Schlichtmann
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : As chip complexity increases and time-to-market pressures grow, front-end design has become a critical bottleneck in chip development. Recently, Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown great potential in Electronic Design Automation (EDA). Beyond specification understanding, LLMs show the potential ...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09616v1

### Mosaic: Runtime-Efficient Multi-Agent Embodied Planning
- **arXiv** : 2607.09603v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-10
- **Auteurs** : Kunjal Panchal, Saayan Mitra, Sunav Choudhary, Victor Bursztyn, Somdeb Sarkhel
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : LLM-based multi-agent embodied planning remains impractical due to prohibitively high execution latency. We identify failed actions as the dominant bottleneck, stemming from two core challenges: inaccurate state tracking under partial observability and inefficient coordination that produces redundan...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09603v1

### TrustX Agent Risk Classification Framework (ARC): Risk-Tiering Internally Created Agentic AI Systems
- **arXiv** : 2607.09586v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-10
- **Auteurs** : Hannah M. Liu, Rhea Saxena, Shiv Asthana
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : The proliferation of agentic AI systems across enterprise and public-sector contexts has outpaced the capacity of general-purpose AI risk frameworks to classify and govern them. In this paper, we introduce the TrustX Agent Risk Classification Framework, a structured, repeatable instrument that can b...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09586v1

### Writing Bug Reports for Software Repair Agents: What Information Matters Most?
- **arXiv** : 2607.09553v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-10
- **Auteurs** : Vincenzo Luigi Bruno, Alessandro Giagnorio, Daniele Bifolco, Leon Wienges, Massimiliano Di Penta
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Software development is increasingly moving toward agentic-first workflows. This includes AI agents responsible for generating initial fixes for submitted issue reports. In this setting, issue reports are no longer merely documentation for human maintainers; they become the primary task specificatio...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09553v1

### Balancing Usefulness and Naturalness: An LLM-based Curation Pipeline for Code Review Comments
- **arXiv** : 2607.09524v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-10
- **Auteurs** : Oussama Ben Sghaier, Martin Weyssow, Houari Sahraoui
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Code review is a cornerstone of software development, where reviewers provide feedback through written comments to ensure code quality, maintainability, and correctness. The effectiveness of this process hinges on the quality of review comments. As large language models (LLMs) gain traction in autom...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09524v1

### Multimodal Reward Hacking in Reinforcement Learning
- **arXiv** : 2607.09492v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-10
- **Auteurs** : Jiayu Yao, Yiwei Wang, Anmeng Zhang, Zhe Sun, Songsong Wang
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Reinforcement learning (RL) is increasingly used to align multimodal large language models (MLLMs), but higher rewards do not always imply better task performance. This risk is amplified when visual evidence is evaluated by text-only or weakly grounded rewards. We study reward hacking in MLLM RL acr...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09492v1

### Neural Collapse Is Forbidden: Information Floors in Language Models
- **arXiv** : 2607.09487v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-10
- **Auteurs** : Bruno Abrahao
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Within-class variance in language-model representations is commonly read as incomplete neural collapse. We argue it is allocated information storage, and that the allocation obeys a law. A one-line centering identity voids a family of simplex equiangular-tight-frame claims, including our own earlier...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09487v1

### Evolution of Accuracy and Visual-Cognitive Errors in a Decade of Vision-Language AI Models
- **arXiv** : 2607.09654v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-10
- **Auteurs** : Shravan Murlidaran, Miguel P. Eckstein
- **Score** : 5
- **Résumé** : Vision language models (VLMs) have made remarkable progress in visual reasoning during the last decade. Most evaluations have used simple scenes (MS-COCO) that do not showcase complex human interactions or behaviors, only a handful of non-curated human descriptions as a benchmark, and have not focus...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09654v1

### The Count Is There, but Misaligned: Understanding and Correcting Counting Failures in VLMs
- **arXiv** : 2607.09544v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-10
- **Auteurs** : Ahmed Oumar El-Shangiti, Abzal Nurgazy, Hilal AlQuabeh, Nikolai Rozanov, Kentaro Inui
- **Score** : 4
- **Résumé** : Despite strong performance on many multimodal tasks, vision-language models (VLMs) still struggle with basic object counting. We investigate whether this reflects missing internal knowledge or a gap between internal representations and verbalized outputs. Training simple probes on activations from f...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09544v1

### Seeing is Free, Speaking is Not: Uncovering the True Energy Bottleneck in Edge VLM Inference
- **arXiv** : 2607.09520v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-10
- **Auteurs** : Junfei Zhan, Haoxun Shen, Mingang Guo, Zixuan Huang, Tengjiao He
- **Score** : 4
- **Résumé** : Vision-Language Models (VLMs) are the perceptual backbone of embodied AI, but their energy footprint on edge hardware remains poorly understood. Existing efficiency efforts focus predominantly on reducing visual tokens, implicitly treating visual processing as the dominant energy cost. We overturn t...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09520v1

### 4DR360: State Reasoning for Joint 3D Detection and Occupancy Prediction in 4D Radar-Camera Full-Scene Perception
- **arXiv** : 2607.09629v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-10
- **Auteurs** : Xiaokai Bai, Lianqing Zheng, Runwei Guan, Songkai Wang, Siyuan Cao
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : Reliable autonomous driving requires full-scene perception that couples foreground objects with dense semantic layout. Recently, 4D millimeter-wave radar has emerged as a robust and affordable sensor, yet its sparse returns make radar-camera fusion necessary for comprehensive scene understanding. Ex...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09629v1

### Task-Specific Multimodal Question Answering Agents via Confidence Calibration and Incremental Reasoning for QANTA 2026
- **arXiv** : 2607.09623v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-10
- **Auteurs** : Nirjhar Das, Md. Al-Mamun Provath
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : We present our submission to the QANTA 2026 shared challenge at the ICML 2026 Workshop on Efficient Multimodal Question Answering (EMM-QA). Quanta evaluates multimodal quizbowl systems that answer pyramid-style questions from incrementally revealed text and accompanying images while operating under ...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09623v1

### Knowledge Graphs and Explainable AI as Complementary Resources for Urban Mining
- **arXiv** : 2607.09578v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-10
- **Auteurs** : Jan Gronewald, Andreas Emrich, Nijat Mehdiyev
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : Pre-demolition assessment, the regulated audit process at the heart of urban mining, is an information process in which AI support must serve qualified auditors who remain accountable for the decisions taken. The relevant unit of value is not prediction accuracy alone, but the defensibility of the s...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09578v1

### TCLA: Training-Free Class-wise Logit Adaptation for Medical Vision-Language Models
- **arXiv** : 2607.09562v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-10
- **Auteurs** : Tianyou Jiang, Ziyu Zhou
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : Medical Vision-Language Models (VLMs) exhibit strong zero-shot performance, yet their effectiveness still declines on out-of-distribution (OOD) data due to domain shifts and class bias inherited from large-scale pretraining. Existing few-shot adaptation methods typically introduce additional trainab...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09562v1

### High-Dimensional Interpolators Can Be Fragile: Heavy Tails and High-Dimensional Large Deviations
- **arXiv** : 2607.09547v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-10
- **Auteurs** : Youheng Zhu, Yiping Lu
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : High-dimensional interpolation is common in modern machine learning, but its tail risk is less understood than its expected prediction risk. Existing theory shows that interpolating models can perform well in expectation, yet such guarantees do not determine the probability of rare, severe errors. I...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09547v1

### ALICE: Learning a General-Purpose Pathology Foundation Model from Vision, Vision-Language, and Slide-Level Experts
- **arXiv** : 2607.09526v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-10
- **Auteurs** : Jiawen Li, Tian Guan, Huijuan Shi, Xitong Ling, Mingxi Fu
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : Foundation models are reshaping computational pathology, yet their capabilities remain shaped by pretraining objectives, data sources, and spatial scales, fragmenting complementary expertise across separate backbones. Here we present ALICE, a unified foundation model trained through multi-stage aggl...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.09526v1

<!-- ARXIV_FEED_END -->
